assertions.description: Board correctly identified as 4-layer (F.Cu, In1.Cu, In2.Cu, B.Cu), 30×53.5mm (standard Eurorack 6HP), 54 footprints (50 BOM + 4 board-only graphics/logos), 306 track segments, 98 vias, 1 GND zone, routing_complete=true, unrouted=0. Stackup details including dielectric thicknesses and FR4 properties are correctly parsed.

U4 TDFN-8 thermal pad (9, 3.82mm², GND net) has 5 standalone vias (effective 8.9 with GND pour fill), density 2.33/mm², adequacy='adequate'. The tenting warning (5 untented vias may cause solder wicking) is also correctly flagged. The recommended minimum of 5 vias is met.
assertions.aspirational_reason: array index path may not match actual output structure

assertions.description: The 4 non-BOM footprints (OSHW-Logo, Winterbloom LOGO, 2× Graphics) are all flagged with board_only=true, exclude_from_bom=true, exclude_from_pos=true, pad_count=0. The BOM footprint count is 50, matching the schematic's 50 components.

The placement_analysis reports 14 courtyard overlaps. The largest (70.09mm²) between RV1 (dual potentiometer) and PS1 (DC-DC converter) is verified as real by coordinate calculation: RV1 courtyard [75.84–88.56, 115.1–131.35] vs PS1 [72.65–85.35, 123.98–139.72] gives x_overlap=9.51mm, y_overlap=7.37mm = 70.1mm². These are design-intentional overlaps common in Eurorack modules where 3D clearances are managed manually.

PCB has 43 nets total, 40 with routing segments, 3 without: unconnected-(PS1-Adj-Pad6), unconnected-(PS1-EN-Pad10), and unconnected-(U2-NC-Pad1). These are intentional no-connect pads: PS1's Adj and EN pins are unused on the V78E05-500-SMT, and U2's (74LVC1G04) NC pin is by definition unconnected. routing_complete=true is correctly reported.

INCORRECT: The connectivity block reports total_nets_with_pads=43, routed_nets=40, unrouted_count=0. The discrepancy (43-40=3) is because 3 nets have only one pad each (the no-connect stubs above). These are not unrouted but rather single-pad nets. The field name 'routed_nets' is misleading — it counts nets that have routing segments, excluding single-pad nets. The combination of unrouted_count=0 and routed_nets≠total_nets is confusing but not strictly wrong given the single-pad explanation.

The board is a standard Eurorack 3U module (30mm width = 6HP, 53.5mm height ≈ 3U). The PCB analyzer reports the raw dimensions but does not classify this as a Eurorack module or verify against the expected Eurorack PCB profile (standard is 30mm wide, height typically 53.5–60mm for 3U modules). The board_metadata section does not contain form-factor classification.
